data.title: Gold Rises on Possible Investment Demand — Market Talk
data.description: Gold rises in the early Asian session on possible investment demand. TD Securities' analysis of '13F' filings for institutional holdings in the biggest physical gold ETF suggests that the breadth of companies buying gold has increased. The data also point to a broadening participation in a consensus gold bull market, says the brokerage's Daniel Ghali in a research report. Moreover, central bank purchases will probably stay as a secular trend, the senior commodity strategist adds. Spot gold is 0.6% higher at $4,102.58/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: GDG: Record growth in revenue, profit, and FUM, cementing market leadership in managed accounts
data.description:
FY25 saw strong revenue and profit growth, driven by acquisitions of Lonsec and Evidentia, and record FUM in investment bonds and managed accounts. Strategic alliances, product innovation, and operational leverage position the group for continued expansion and market leadership.

Original document: Generation Development Group Limited [GDG] Slides Release — Nov. 19 2025

data.title: Shareholder Lock-in: Shares worth ₹410 crore of four stokcs free-up today; three below IPO price
data.description:
Four stocks, Vikram Solar Ltd., Shreeji Shipping Ltd., Gem Aromatics Ltd., and Patel Retail Ltd. will be seeing their respective three-month shareholder lock-in period end on Thursday, November 20.

According to Nuvama Alternative & Quantitative Research, 1.5 crore shares of these four stocks will become eligible for trade with the end of the lock-in period. Based on their respective Current Market Price, the value of the shares that become eligible for trade is worth nearly ₹410 crore.

It must be noted that the end of the shareholder lock-in period does not mean all the shares will be sold in the open market. These shares will only become eligible to be traded. Lets look at the four stocks whose lock-in period is ending.

Vikram Solar

According to Nuvama Alternative, 93 lakh shares or 3% of Vikram Solar's outstanding equity will become eligible to trade from Thursday. The three-month lock-in period ends in today's session.

Based on Wednesday's closing price, the total amount of Vikram Solar shares that become eligible to be traded is worth ₹275.7 crore.

The stock, on Wednesday, closed below its IPO price of ₹332 per share.

Shreeji Shipping

The stock will also see its three-month shareholder lock-in period end on Wednesday. According to Nuvama Alternative, 24 lakh shares worth ₹75 crore will become eligible to be traded on Thursday.

The number of shares that will become eligible to be traded amount to 2% of the company's outstanding equity.

As of Wednesday's closing, the stock is up 23% from its issue price of ₹252 per share.

Patel Retail

The company will see a total of 9 lakh shares or 3% of its outstanding equity free up for trade on Thursday when its three-month shareholder lock-in ends.

The total value of these shares, based on Wednesday's close, is worth ₹20 crore.

Shares of Patel Retail are trading 14.5% below their issue price of ₹255 per share.

Gem Aromatics

Another underperforming IPO, Gem Aromatics will see 21 lakh shares free up for trade on Thursday as its three-month shareholder lock-in ends. The total number of shares freeing up for trade amount to 4% of the outstanding equity.

The value of the shares that free up for trade is worth ₹37.4 crore.

Shares of Gem Aromatics are down 45% from their IPO price of ₹325 per share.

data.title: China, Kyrgyzstan looking at further cooperation in infrastructure, agriculture and minerals
data.description:
China and Kyrgyzstan discussed further economic cooperation, including building border port and highway infrastructure, developing agricultural trade and exploring green minerals, according to a joint statement released by the Chinese foreign ministry on Thursday.

Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi and his counterpart Jeenbek Kulubayev met on Wednesday in Kyrgyz capital Bishkek for talks focusing on economic and trade matters, connectivity, politics and regional issues, the statement said.

To help bilateral trade, both sides discussed opening border crossings, speeding up construction of the Bederi border port and modernising two existing ones in Kyrgyzstan, and improving customs clearance capacity.

They also discussed opening an international air corridor in southern Kyrgyzstan, a poorer region of the Central Asian country, and adding direct flights between China's northwestern city of Kashgar with Bishkek and Osh.

The ministers spoke about developing agricultural trade and support for cooperation in the green minerals field, but did not elaborate on the latter.

Wang also met with Kyrgyz President Sadyr Japarov, according to the Chinese foreign ministry.

data.title: Lenovo reports a 5% fall in Q2 profit
data.description:
By Che Pan and Brenda Goh

China's Lenovo on Thursday reported a 5% drop in second-quarter profit.

The world's largest personal computer maker posted revenue of $20.5 billion for the three months ended September 30, ahead of analysts' expectations of $20.3 billion, according to LSEG data.

Net profit attributable to shareholders was $340 million, below the consensus estimate of $449.4 million.

data.title: Lenovo 2Q Revenue Rises on Strong AI Push
data.description:
(23:40 GMT) *Lenovo Group 2Q Rev $20.45B Vs. $17.85B >0992.HK
(23:41 GMT) *Lenovo Group 2Q Net $340.0M Vs. Net $359.0M >0992.HK
(23:41 GMT) *Lenovo Group 2Q Adj Net $512.0M Vs. $409.0M >0992.HK
(23:59 GMT) Lenovo 2Q Revenue Rises on Strong AI Push

By P.R. Venkat

Lenovo Group's revenue rose sharply in the second quarter, benefiting from the global push to adopt Windows 11 and the world's largest PC maker's growing server business amid the artificial-intelligence boom.

Revenue for the three months ended Sept. 30 increased 15% from a year earlier, to $20.45 billion, marginally topping market expectation of $20 billion.

Net profit fell 5% on year, to $340 million, missing the $382 million expected in a FactSet poll of analysts.

However, its adjusted net profit, excluding non-cash and non-operating effects, rose 25%, to $512.0 million.

Lenovo's main business, the intelligent-devices segment that includes PCs, smartphones and tablets, posted a 15% rise in revenue in the first half.

The global PC market has maintained strong momentum, helped by the Windows 11 transition.

Shipments of PCs in the September quarter rose 17%, to 19.4 million units, with a market share of 25.5%, according to IDC data.

Write to P.R. Venkat at venkat.pr@wsj.com

data.title: New World's debt recast is just the beginning
data.description:
By Ka Sing Chan

The crisis for New World Development is far from over. The Hong Kong property developer narrowly escaped its own “China Evergrande moment” in July when it secured a $11 billion refinancing with banks. Now the distressed group controlled by the Cheng family is trimming its balance sheet with a small debt swap. It's a step in the right direction but a much deeper restructuring may be hard to avoid.

In a voluntary plan outlined this month, New World proposed to issue up to $1.9 billion of new debt in exchange for existing perpetuals and senior notes worth more than $3 billion. Although the offer requires bondholders to accept haircuts of as much as 50%, the new notes have higher coupons and improved security linked to the company's Victoria Dockside complex, location of the Rosewood, which recently took the number one ranking in the world's best hotels. Initial acceptances of the offer, the company said on Tuesday, will result in a $1.3 billion net reduction.

The mini-rejig leaves much to be desired, however, including for HSBC, Bank of China and other lenders that backed its refinancing. Hong Kong’s commercial real estate prices have fallen more than 50% since 2021. Several of the city's weaker developers have already defaulted. Yet New World is sitting on about $20 billion worth of borrowings, of which $8.8 billion will be due in three years. It has more net debt, including perpetuals, than rivals Wharf , Henderson Land Development and Li Ka-shing's CK Asset combined.

That leaves New World under pressure. Options for asset sales are limited now that it has pledged most of its best developments against borrowings. The Chengs were planning to inject up to $1.3 billion into their listed property flagship, Bloomberg reported in August, citing unnamed people familiar with the matter, and were seeking a partner to provide a similar amount as a capital infusion.

Absent a broad-based recovery in the property market, New World has its work cut out.

CONTEXT NEWS

New World Development on November 18 said that initial acceptances of the company’s offer to restructure its outstanding perpetual notes and securities will result in a $1.3 billion net reduction of debt.

The Hong Kong developer was proposing to issue new instruments worth more than $1.9 billion, implying more than $3 billion that could have been tendered. The final deadline for acceptances is in early December.

The swaps will result in an effective haircut of around 50%. The company said the new instruments will have “an enhanced credit profile that is linked to Victoria Dockside”, referring to a commercial complex in Hong Kong's prime shopping district Tsim Sha Tsui.

In June, New World refinanced HK$88.2 billion ($11.3 billion) of bank debt and in September it secured a fresh facility for an initial committed tranche of roughly HK$4 billion, secured by a “first-ranking mortgage” over Victoria Dockside.

data.title: Napier Port's Earnings Guidance Looks Conservative Again — Market Talk
data.description: Napier Port has a good shot at beating its annual earnings guidance, suggests Forsyth Barr. Napier Port expects FY 2026 Ebitda of NZ$70 million-NZ$74 million. That implies growth of 9%-15% on year. Management has typically been conservative with its earnings projections. "And we believe this guidance follows that pattern," says analyst Andy Bowley. Recent tariff schedule changes to the container infrastructure levy and vehicle booking system charges suggest another strong year of unit revenue growth. That should underwrite another year of strong profit growth, Forsyth Barr says. It retains an outperform call on Napier Port and raises its price target by 17% to NZ$4.10/share. Napier Port is unchanged at NZ$3.51. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Fitch Ratings to Grant Equity Credit for Japan Insurance Holding Companies’ Tier 2 Senior Debt
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Tokyo/Hong Kong-19 November 2025: Fitch Ratings will assign equity credit to Tier 2 senior debt issued by Japanese insurance holding companies at the group level from 31 March 2026.

Under Japan’s new economic value-based insurance solvency regime, effective 31 March 2026, senior debt issued at the insurance holding company level will be recognised as Tier 2 capital, provided the proceeds are downstreamed to the insurance operating company as subordinated debt or instruments of lower seniority.

To qualify as Tier 2 capital under Japan’s Financial Services Agency (FSA) framework, the senior debt must include a provision requiring the issuer to meet the FSA’s Minimum Capital Requirement (MCR), defined as an Economic Solvency Ratio (ESR), based on Japan’s Insurance Capital Standard, of 100% at maturity. If the MCR is not met, principal repayment is deferred until the requirement is satisfied—commonly referred to as a “lock-in clause.”

Under Japan’s approach, proceeds from such debt must be downstreamed to the operating insurance company as subordinated debt or instruments of lower seniority to qualify as Tier 2 capital. These subordinated instruments are then treated as 100% equity capital in the Fitch Prism Global model score—both at the operating company level and at the group consolidated level—in line with the agency’s “regulatory override” principle outlined in its Insurance Rating Criteria. However, this Tier 2 senior debt is classified as 100% debt for the agency's financial leverage ratio and total financing and commitments ratio calculations.

Fitch's Issuer Default Rating (IDR) on a Japanese insurance holding company is likely to be aligned with the IDR of its operating company, because Japan is classified as following a ‘Group Solvency’ framework. The senior debt issued by an insurance holding company is rated one notch below the holding company IDR, because the recovery assumption of this senior debt is deemed ‘Below Average’, similar to that of an insurance operating company’s subordinated debt, per Fitch’s Insurance Rating Criteria.

The final notching applied to the Tier 2 senior debt will be determined on a case-by-case basis, based on a comprehensive assessment under our criteria, especially when the features of the holding company’s debt are more complex than standard instruments.

data.title: Jack in the Box 4Q Profit Slides on Lower Sales — Update
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Jack in the Box logged lower profit and sales in its latest quarter, hurt by a decrease in transactions.

The first few weeks of the quarter started off rocky, though sales trends improved as the company introduced more promotions to draw value-conscious consumers, Chief Executive Lance Tucker said on a call with analysts.

"Our category is more competitive than ever, and consumers are very careful about where they spend," Tucker said.

Going forward, the company plans to continue to focus on offering a variety of price points, particularly at the low end.

"That is not the place where we want to build all our sales. It's not the place where we want to drive the business," Tucker said. "But we do recognize that we've got to consistently be there with some fresh innovation and some, frankly, some good value, literally every window every week."

The fast-food chain on Wednesday posted a fourth-quarter profit of $5.8 million, or 30 cents a share, compared with $21.9 million, or $1.12 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were 30 cents, missing estimates of 45 cents a share, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Revenue fell 6.6% to $326.2 million. Analysts expected $318.7 million.

Jack in the Box same-store sales slid 7.4% in the quarter, while the company's Del Taco locations reported a same-store sales decline of 3.9%. The decreases were driven by lower transactions and mix, which more than offset higher prices, the company said.

For the current fiscal year, the company forecasts a decline in Jack in the Box same-store sales of down 1% to up 1%. It expects results will remain pressured in the first quarter and improve during the rest of the year.

Last month, Jack in the Box said it signed a deal to sell its Del Taco unit to privately held restaurant operator Yadav Enterprises for $115 million in cash. The segment's results will be reflected in discontinued operations for the fiscal year, and are not included in guidance, the company said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Nasdaq futures surge over 300 points after strong Nvidia results; All eyes on jobs report
data.description:
Futures on Wall Street have surged higher on Thursday, November 20, in response to the blockbuster results reported by Nvidia Corp. which has assuaged investor fears about a potential AI bubble.

Gains are being led by the Nasdaq futures, which are up 325 points, followed by the Dow futures, which are up 160 points, while the S&P 500 futures are trading with gains of 60 points.

Nvidia Corp beat expectations on both revenue and the Earnings Per Share (EPS) front. The company's guidance for the ongoing quarter was also higher than analyst expectations, sending the stock higher by over 5% in extended trading.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang also addressed concerns regarding a potential AI bubble on the company's earnings call. "From our vantage point, we see something very different," he said. Concerns over AI valuations have been the biggest factor in the recent Wall Street sell-off, with the Dow Jones falling over 2,000 points in the four sessions prior to Wednesday.

In response to Nvidia's results, shares of other chipmakers, Broadcom, Qualcomm, AMD and TSMC have also gained between 2.5% to 4% in extended trading on Wall Street.

Benchmark indices were mainly in wait-and-watch mode in regular trading on Wednesday ahead of Nvidia's results, which were reported after the closing bell. The Dow ended above the flat line, putting and end to the four-day losing streak, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq gained 0.4% and 0.6% respectively.

All eyes are now on the second big news trigger for the week, which is the Non-farm payrolls data for September, which will be the first major official statistic reported after the end to the longest government shutdown in US history. Analysts are projecting an addition of 55,000 jobs for the month compared to August. The unemployment rate may or may not be reported along with the jobs report.

Elsewhere, Fed minutes released last evening showed "many" officials stating that there is no need to cut rates further, further enforcing signs of a divided central bank ahead of the December 10 policy decision. As of Thursday morning, the probability of the Fed easing by 25 basis points next month fell to 33%, from 42% previously, according to the CME FedWatch.

In light of the dwindling chances of a Fed rate cut next month, the US Dollar index gained the most since September, rising back above the mark of 100, while Gold prices remain below the $4,100 mark, but are not seeing any steep fall.

data.title: Corn, soy basis steady to firmer as farmer sales slow
data.description:
Basis bids for corn and soybeans were steady to stronger in the U.S. Midwest on Wednesday, reflecting a slowing pace of farmer offerings as Chicago Board of Trade futures backed down from multi-month highs set in recent days, merchandisers said.

  • In the eastern Midwest, the corn basis firmed by 10 cents a bushel at Toledo, Ohio, and by 3 cents at rail terminals in Columbus, Ohio, and Evansville, Indiana. To the west, the corn basis firmed by 5 cents at a Blair, Nebraska, processing site and by 2 cents at an elevator in Council Bluffs, Iowa.

  • For soybeans, the basis improved by 10 cents at the Toledo port and by 5 cents at Council Bluffs, Iowa. Basis bids were unchanged at soy crushing plants, spot checks showed.

  • The soy basis held steady at grain elevators along Midwest rivers after firming earlier this week in response to demand from exporters at the Gulf. Shippers had sought fresh supplies to cover a series of U.S. soybean sales to China, including multiple cargoes from the Gulf.

  • The U.S. Department of Agriculture confirmed sales of 330,000 metric tons of U.S. soybeans to China, a day after confirming sales to China of 792,000 tons of the oilseed.

  • China had bought at least four cargoes of U.S. soybeans on Tuesday night, including three from U.S. Gulf terminals, two China-based traders with knowledge of the deals said.

  • CBOT January soybean futures (SF26) settled down 17-1/4 cents on Wednesday at $11.36-1/4 per bushel and December corn (CZ25) fell 7 cents to end at $4.29-3/4 per bushel.

data.title: BHP Vicuna JV Offers Significant Copper Output, At a Cost — Market Talk
data.description: BHP's Vicuna joint venture has the potential to boost the miner's copper production by roughly 200,000 metric tons annually, "but will require substantial capital investment to do so," Jefferies analysts say in a note. They estimate it will cost US$10.5 billion-US$14 billion to sequentially develop the Josemaría, Filo del Sol oxides and Filo del Sol sulphides owned by the JV, in which BHP has a 50% stake. The analysts expect first output from 2032 and that the JV will ramp up to a capacity around 450,000 tons by 2043. The development offsets declining production from BHP's existing Chilean assets, they say. However, rising capex will likely weigh on free cash flow and lead to "a downward trend in capital returns between now and FY30," they add.(rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Jack in the Box Sees Consumers on the Hunt for Value — Market Talk
data.description: Jack in the Box CEO Lance Tucker says consumers are increasingly looking for value. The fast-food chain's latest quarter got a rocky start, but sales improved as the company rejiggered its promotions to draw cautious consumers, he says during a call with analysts. "Our category is more competitive than ever, and consumers are very careful about where they spend," he says. In the current quarter, the company has moved to make its signature combos more affordable and increased cup sizes on its small combos, he says. "While we know these changes won't improve results overnight, we are taking necessary steps to enhance how we improve our value perception," he says. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Tethys Petroleum Press Release
data.description:
Corporate update

Grand Cayman, Cayman Islands--(Newsfile Corp. - November 19, 2025) - Tethys Petroleum Limited ("Tethys" or the "Company") is pleased to provide an update on the Company's operations in the Republic of Kazakhstan.

Legal update

The Company had previously reported that an arbitration ruling has been made on May 13, 2025 ordering DSFK to pay the Company approximately 1.4 billion KZT and for 18 million shares of the Company held by Olisol Petroleum Limited to be cancelled. DSFK and owners of Olisol challenged the final award. The AIFC Court directed that the hearing on these challenges be conducted on November 7, 2025. The Court issued its judgment on November 17. All claims of DSFK, Olisol and its owners were rejected and the court confirmed that the award is to be enforced. Tethys is moving forward on the next steps to enforce the judgment.

Non-Binding Letter of intent to acquire the Company update

On September 15, 2025 Fincraft Group LLP announced that it had submitted a non-binding letter of intent to the Board of Directors of Tethys in respect of a proposed transaction pursuant to which Fincraft would acquire all of the issued and outstanding ordinary shares of Tethys at a price of 1.38 CAD per share. Fincraft Group LLP has provided an update to their proposal indicating that they are prepared to purchase all of the Tethys Shares not already owned by Fincraft at a purchase price of $1.75 CAD per share. The Special Committee of independent Directors is reviewing the updated proposal and is in the process of engaging with the Fincraft Group LLP.

About Tethys

Tethys is focused on oil and gas exploration and production activities in Central Asia.

Disclaimer

Some of the statements in this document are forward-looking. No part of this announcement constitutes, or shall be taken to constitute, an invitation or inducement to invest in the Company or any other entity and shareholders of the Company are cautioned not to place undue reliance on the forward-looking statements. Save as required by applicable law, the Company does not undertake to update or change any forward-looking statements to reflect events occurring after the date of this announcement.

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Contact Information:

  • Tethys PetroleumCasey McCandlessChief Financial Officer
  • info@tethys-group.comwww.tethys-group.com
